message.content:
Adversaries may exploit legitimate external web services for one-way communication to send commands to compromised systems without receiving return output over the same channel. This technique leverages popular websites and social media platforms, such as Google, Twitter, GitHub, and TechNet, to host command and control (C2) instructions, providing significant cover due to the routine communication with these services within networks. The use of SSL/TLS encryption by these web services further obscures malicious activities. Notable examples include EVILNUM using GitLab and Digital Point, HAMMERTOSS utilizing Twitter and GitHub, Leviathan leveraging user profiles on legitimate websites, Metamorfo downloading zip files, and OnionDuke using Twitter as a backup C2. 

The potential impact includes covert command execution and data exfiltration without detection. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and analyzing network traffic for unusual patterns, implementing strict web filtering policies, and employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and block suspicious activities. Regularly updating threat intelligence and employing behavioral analytics can also help in detecting and mitigating such threats.
